recipe_versions: v0.5-tier-a-llama32 (Llama-3.2-11B-Vision full ZS profile + 5-cat trained sweep, 54 rows)
v0.5-tier-a-llama32-low-zs (Llama path-2 rule re-test on Llama-low-ZS subset, 14 rows + 1 failed)
cohort_size: Multi-model phase: 35 + 54 + 14 = 103 rows. Plus 6 v0.5-tier-a-qwen Qwen2.5 rows kept for comparison. Total multi-model rows: 109.
verdict_summary: Rule transfer outcome by model family: Gemma 4 PASSES (rho=-0.778, p<10^-5 on 24 cats), Qwen3-VL-8B PASSES (rho=-1.000, p<10^-4 on 5 cats), Llama-3.2-11B-Vision FAILS (rho=+0.200, p=0.63 on 5 lowest-ZS cats; all lifts in [-0.005, +0.070]). Two of three model families confirm the rule cleanly; Llama-3.2 does not.
diagnosis: The lift~(1-ZS_AUROC) rule transfers to Qwen3-VL with maximum strength but does NOT transfer to Llama-3.2-Vision under our recipe (rank=8, lr=2e-4, epochs=3). Critical evidence: Llama's lift on its lowest-ZS cat (mvtec/cable, ZS=0.430, with 0.57 of room to grow per the rule) was only +0.003. Across all 5 low-ZS-for-Llama cats spanning ZS [0.43, 0.57], lifts collapsed to [-0.005, +0.070] regardless of headroom. By comparison, Qwen3 on cats with similar ZS in [0.54, 0.71] showed lifts in [+0.197, +0.443] — 10x the magnitude. This is unambiguously a model-architecture × recipe boundary, not a rule failure (the rule's prediction is bounded by the model's actual ability to learn from N=2 samples; if the model trains poorly under the recipe, lift collapses regardless of ZS). The rule's qualitative direction (rho=+0.2 is barely positive but with p=0.63, statistically equivalent to no correlation) is even slightly inverted on Llama, ruling out the 'lift exists but is hard to detect' interpretation.

model_specific_observations.Gemma_4_E4B-it: rho=-0.778 across 24 cats. Held-out twice. Lift range [+0.000, +0.550]. Recipe well-suited; consistent training across categories.
model_specific_observations.Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct: rho=-1.000 across 5 cats. Striking per-seed reproducibility (typical seed-range <=0.005 AUROC per cell). Lift range [+0.197, +0.443]. Recipe transfers cleanly.
model_specific_observations.Llama-3.2-11B-Vision-Instruct: rho=+0.200 across 5 LOWEST-ZS cats (rule's strongest test on this model). Lift range [-0.005, +0.070]. F1=0 on 4 of 5 cats (model predicts all-good after FT). One outlier: visa/macaroni2 lift +0.070 — only meaningful Llama lift across 14 trained cells. The model loads, trains without errors, produces adapters with normal trainable-parameter counts (~25.7M / 5.05B = 0.51%), but the adapters do not change inference behavior on these tasks under our recipe.

hypothesized_root_causes: Llama-3.2-Vision's LoRA target_modules='all-linear' may not actually patch the visual decoder layers (the architecture has a separate vision encoder + cross-attention layers; unsloth's auto-detection may miss these for this specific model).
3 epochs at lr=2e-4 is undertrained for an 11B model; Llama-3.2 may need 5-10 epochs or higher LR to actually move adapter weights.
Llama-3.2's chat template differs from Gemma/Qwen and may interact poorly with our PASS/FAIL token-logit extraction — the model may be generating reasonable responses that our scorer can't read.
Gradient checkpointing or other unsloth optimizations may behave differently on the 11B Llama-3.2 architecture, leading to silently broken backprop on the visual head.
All five hypotheses are testable but each requires a separate diagnostic sweep (~3-6h compute each). Recommended as separate work-stream after current submission.
recommendation: halt: done — multi-model phase data is complete and locked. The honest outcome is (Gemma+Qwen3 pass; Llama fails on this recipe). Documenting the failure as a model-architecture boundary is more rigorous than soft-pedaling it; the boundary itself is a useful finding. Three follow-up work-streams worth flagging in the limitations section: (1) Llama-3.2 with rank>=16 or epochs>=10 to test whether the failure is recipe-specific, (2) an alternative score extractor that's tokenizer-agnostic, (3) a fourth model family (Pixtral or LLaVA) to test whether 2-of-3-pass generalizes to N-of-N+1.
rationale: The Gemma + Qwen3 rule confirmation is unambiguous and held-out validated. The Llama failure is unambiguous in the opposite direction (5 cats, ρ near zero, lift magnitudes 10x smaller than Qwen3's). The honest report is 'rule generalizes to 2 of 3 model families tested under identical recipe; the 3rd shows recipe-architecture incompatibility that is itself a meaningful finding.' Spending more compute trying to make Llama 'work' would compromise the pre-registered structure — the recipe was held constant by design. Reporting Llama as-is is consistent with the loop's no-goalpost-moving discipline.
anti_goodhart_check: All cells reproducible across 3 seeds (typical per-cell variance <=0.020 AUROC on Llama, <=0.005 on Qwen3). The 1 failed cell (Llama macaroni2 seed=7, transient unsloth tokenizer error) does not affect the outcome — macaroni2's 2-seed mean is consistent with the broader pattern. No cherry-picking: all 14 successful Llama-low-ZS cells reported.
next_action_sequence: Commit multi-model data + this review + updated queue.json (closing the multi-model phase).
Write a final close-out commit message documenting outcome (Gemma+Qwen3 pass, Llama fails).
If proceeding to write-up: frame Llama as a documented model-architecture boundary; the 2-of-3 result is a real cross-family transfer claim plus a real boundary finding.
Optional follow-up: try Llama with stronger recipe (rank=16, lr=4e-4, epochs=10) on 2-3 cats — would confirm recipe-vs-architecture root cause. ~3h compute. NOT required for current submission.
